dimension_notes_summary.cross_module_architecture: Dependency direction is strictly layered: tools/ → toolkit/ (core) → never reverse. Coupling matrix shows tools/ imports toolkit/ 34 times; toolkit/ imports tools/ 9 times (index.ts re-exports only —
dimension_notes_summary.high_level_elegance: The toolkit directory structure maps cleanly to domain capabilities: tools/ (user-facing operations), guards/ (invariant enforcement), core files (types, session, schemas, state-store, sdk-bridge, url
dimension_notes_summary.convention_outlier: All files use camelCase for functions and variables, PascalCase for types/interfaces/classes — consistent with TypeScript conventions. 141 function names sampled, all follow the same convention.
dimension_notes_summary.error_consistency: The toolkit has a documented two-strategy model: tools return ToolResult<T> (ok/err envelope); internal helpers and SDK bridge throw. This layering is intentional and consistent with the 'Result in co
dimension_notes_summary.naming_quality: Guard functions consistently use 'checkAndRecord*' pattern: checkAndRecordDedup, checkAndRecordWrite, checkAndRecordTip — clear, intent-bearing compound names that communicate both read and write sema
dimension_notes_summary.abstraction_fitness: withToolWrapper<T> centralizes 5 boilerplate concerns (timing, touch, onToolCall notification, error wrapping, DemosError passthrough) used by 7 tools — clear leverage with no pass-through
dimension_notes_summary.dependency_health: 5 production dependencies: @anthropic-ai/sdk, @kynesyslabs/demosdk, proper-lockfile, yaml, zod — lean set for the scope
dimension_notes_summary.low_level_elegance: Guards uniformly use checkAndAppend with a closure for the predicate — each guard's check logic is a pure inline function: clear, bounded, local state mutation (prune + maybe append) in one place
dimension_notes_summary.mid_level_elegance: withToolWrapper pattern in tool-wrapper.ts cleanly extracts cross-cutting concerns (timing, touch, onToolCall, error wrapping) with a single consistent calling convention across 7+ tools
dimension_notes_summary.test_strategy: 21 test files spanning 24 production files — near 1:1 test-to-module ratio, strong coverage intent
dimension_notes_summary.api_surface_coherence: All tools except discoverSources share a consistent calling convention: fn(session: DemosSession, opts: XxxOptions) => Promise<ToolResult<XxxResult>>
dimension_notes_summary.authorization_consistency: No route handlers, HTTP middleware, or RLS tables exist — this is a toolkit, not a web server. Auth consistency must be evaluated at the trust boundary layer: session gating, SSRF prevention, spend-ca
dimension_notes_summary.ai_generated_debt: tool-wrapper.ts: docstring at lines 11-17 enumerates 5 numbered steps for a 26-line function that speaks for itself — the comment adds ceremony over insight
dimension_notes_summary.incomplete_migration: write-rate-limit.ts: checkAndRecordWrite() is the new unified API (line 24); checkWriteRateLimit() and recordWrite() are labeled '(backward compat)' wrappers forwarding to it (lines 55-59, 62-78). The
dimension_notes_summary.package_organization: Structure data: 3 directories with clear, non-ambiguous names: src/toolkit/ (core types + session + state store + schemas + url-validator + barrel), src/toolkit/guards/ (rate limiting, dedup, spend ca
dimension_notes_summary.initialization_coupling: connect.ts line 252: 'let _isContainer: boolean | null = null' — module-level mutable singleton that caches detectContainer() result. Explicitly documented as 'cached — container status is invariant p

dimension_notes_summary.contract_coherence: PublishDraft.attestUrl is typed as optional (attestUrl?: string) in types.ts line 129, but executePublishPipeline() throws 'PublishDraft.attestUrl is required' at runtime (publish.ts line 84). The typ
dimension_notes_summary.logic_clarity: verify.ts lines 59-62: return err(demosError('CONFIRM_TIMEOUT', 'Verification exhausted all retries', true), ...) after the for-loop is dead code. The loop iterates attempt 0..RETRY_DELAYS_MS.length (
dimension_notes_summary.type_safety: types.ts: Every public option type (ConnectOptions, PublishDraft, ReplyOptions, ReactOptions, TipOptions, ScanOptions, VerifyOptions, AttestOptions, DiscoverSourcesOptions, PayOptions, TipPolicy, PayP

review_issue_summaries.summary: connect.ts dynamically imports src/lib/auth.js — toolkit tool crossing into app lib layer
Module-level mutable Map cache in discover-sources.ts creates shared process state
discoverSources() accepts nullable session breaking the uniform tool API contract
D402RequirementSchema defined in pay.ts breaks schemas.ts ownership boundary
Guards expose old check/record pairs alongside new unified checkAndRecord — ambiguous API for consumers
discover-sources.ts defines a local SourceStatus type that duplicates the exported SourceStatus from types.ts
scan.ts silently swallows Skill Dojo fallback error, losing all context on dual-path failure
loadCatalog() calls JSON.parse without local try/catch, relying on outer function's catch in different scope
tip-spend-cap and pay-spend-cap check policy bounds before validating isFinite — relies on NaN comparison behavior
Backward-compat guard aliases (checkDedup, checkWriteRateLimit, checkTipSpendCap) create dual naming surface alongside current checkAndRecord* names
discoverSources() manually replicates withToolWrapper boilerplate (touch + onToolCall) for a session-optional tool
Dead-code err() fallback after retry loop in verify.ts is never reached but looks load-bearing
tip() fetches full feed (50 posts) to resolve one post author — implicit, unscalable, and fragile for non-recent posts
identifyOpportunities() in scan.ts uses unexplained magic numbers for reaction threshold and score
SdkBridge stored opaquely inside signingHandle requiring runtime cast in getBridge()
DEFAULT_SUPERCOLONY_API hardcoded in connect.ts, not threaded from ConnectOptions
reply() validates input then delegates to publish(), which re-validates and runs withToolWrapper; reply's own validation errors use latencyMs: 0 instead of wrapper timing
Stale TODO comment in attest.ts claims SDK bridge wiring is pending, but it is already wired
6-way txHash extraction fallback in publishHivePost encodes SDK response shape uncertainty as inline glue
state-helpers.ts (7 importers, shared locking primitive) has no direct tests
discover-sources.ts (109 LOC, highest signal density) has no direct tests; deprecated symbols and unsafe JSON.parse are uncovered
verify.ts retry logic (3s/5s/10s delays, exhaustion path) has no targeted tests; integration test uses real sleep with 25s timeout
Integration test assertions collapse to near-tautologies on DNS failure branch
discoverSources(session: DemosSession | null) is the only tool with a nullable session — breaks uniform calling convention
discoverSources manually reimplements withToolWrapper cross-cutting logic, creating observability inconsistency
DiscoverSourcesOptions.matchThreshold is accepted by discoverSources but never used internally
connect() and disconnect() break the async-ToolResult pattern without documenting the exception in public types
urlAllowlist is stored in DemosSession but never consumed by validateUrl — allow-list control is wired but dead
Inline comments in checkAndAppend restate the code rather than explaining rationale
Four guard recordX() functions duplicate load→prune→push→set instead of reusing checkAndAppend
SourceStatus type and VALID_STATUSES set re-declared locally in discover-sources.ts despite canonical export in types.ts
Unified checkAndRecord* API exists in guards but is not exported — callers forced to use backward-compat wrappers
Module-level catalogCache in discover-sources.ts has no flush mechanism — tests sharing the same module instance cannot isolate catalog contents
apiCall() catch block silently converts network exceptions to ok:false response — callers cannot distinguish DNS failure from HTTP 500
connect() mixes 5 distinct responsibilities in one function body: security checks, credential parsing, SDK connection, auth, session construction
PublishDraft.attestUrl typed optional but throws at runtime if absent — type annotation lies to callers
verify() returns blockHeight: 1 hardcoded — callers receive fabricated data as if it were real chain data
SdkBridge.signAndBroadcast() exposed in interface but no tool uses it and txData shape is untyped unknown
verify() has unreachable return err() after the retry for-loop — loop always returns on final iteration
pay-spend-cap.ts checks amount > maxPerCall before isFinite/positive validation — inverted guard order
txModule parameter typed with bare Function — call signatures are unchecked at every use site
cachedTxModule and cachedD402Client typed as any — type information lost on cache-hit path
discover-sources.ts defines a local SourceStatus type that exactly duplicates the exported type in types.ts
connectSdk() return type has demos: any — Demos instance loses type information at connection site

payload.strategy_summary: Execute 12 clusters in priority order: feed-normalization > guard-debt > type-safety > error-contract > dead-code > elegance-fixes > contract-fixes > test-quality > naming-fixes > boundary-cleanup > abstraction-cleanup > dependency-cleanup. Parallelize independent clusters (guard-debt + dead-code + elegance-fixes can run concurrently). Target: strict 85+ after first 6 clusters.

cluster_summaries.name: feed-normalization
cluster_summaries.thesis: Extract shared feed parser from 3 implementations in scan.ts:78, verify.ts:76, tip.ts:45

cluster_summaries.name: guard-debt
cluster_summaries.thesis: Add @deprecated JSDoc to 6 compat exports in dedup-guard.ts, write-rate-limit.ts, tip-spend-cap.ts, index.ts

cluster_summaries.name: error-contract
cluster_summaries.thesis: Document connect() throws, standardize allowlist guard paths

cluster_summaries.name: dead-code
cluster_summaries.thesis: Remove stale TODOs, dead returns, unused stubs

cluster_summaries.name: elegance-fixes
cluster_summaries.thesis: Magic numbers, schema placement, reply double-wrap, verify limit

cluster_summaries.name: abstraction-cleanup
cluster_summaries.thesis: Inline unnecessary wrappers, narrow session parameters

cluster_summaries.name: contract-fixes
cluster_summaries.thesis: Fix provenance hash, getter side effects, parseWallet guard

cluster_summaries.name: boundary-cleanup
cluster_summaries.thesis: Auth boundary cross, catalog cache scope, container singleton

cluster_summaries.name: naming-fixes
cluster_summaries.thesis: Fix naming mismatches in guards and SDK methods

cluster_summaries.name: test-quality
cluster_summaries.thesis: Fix tautological assertions, real sleep, missing coverage

cluster_summaries.name: type-safety
cluster_summaries.thesis: Create DemosRpcMethods interface in sdk-bridge.ts, replace 4x (demos as any) casts at lines 148, 272, 329, 335

dimension_notes_summary.cross_module_architecture: connect.ts dynamically imports ../../lib/auth.js at two call sites — this is a live cross-boundary dependency from src/toolkit/ into src/lib/, violating the toolkit's documented framework-agnostic bou
dimension_notes_summary.high_level_elegance: Top-level toolkit decomposition maps well to domain: tools/ = capabilities, guards/ = cross-cutting policies, session.ts = lifecycle, types.ts = contracts, schemas.ts = validation, sdk-bridge.ts = ext
dimension_notes_summary.convention_outlier: All tool files use named exports only — consistent across scan, publish, verify, tip, pay, attest, react, connect, discover-sources, feed-parser
dimension_notes_summary.error_consistency: All tools return ToolResult<T> with typed DemosError — consistent error envelope pattern across publish, scan, verify, tip, pay, attest, react, discoverSources
dimension_notes_summary.naming_quality: Tool names are precise and domain-aligned: publish, reply, react, tip, scan, verify, attest, discoverSources, pay — all describe the exact network operation performed
dimension_notes_summary.abstraction_fitness: StateStore interface has exactly one production implementation (FileStateStore), with no adapter pressure or polymorphic substitution in the codebase — but it IS used as a seam for test injection, whi
dimension_notes_summary.dependency_health: package.json production dependencies: @anthropic-ai/sdk, @kynesyslabs/demosdk, proper-lockfile, yaml, zod — all 5 are actively used in production src/toolkit/ code.
dimension_notes_summary.low_level_elegance: pay.ts executePublishPipeline is 9 sequential steps, well-named and comment-delineated. Each step has one purpose. Control flow is direct.
dimension_notes_summary.mid_level_elegance: withToolWrapper is the canonical seam pattern: all 8 tool files call it with (session, toolName, errorCode, fn) — consistent, explicit, and boundary-respecting. The seam design is excellent.
dimension_notes_summary.test_strategy: 25 test files in tests/toolkit/ covering all major toolkit modules. 30 test files total across tests/toolkit/ per glob.
dimension_notes_summary.api_surface_coherence: All 10 public tools follow identical shape: (session: DemosSession, opts: XxxOptions) => Promise<ToolResult<XxxResult>>. Highly consistent parameter ordering.
dimension_notes_summary.authorization_consistency: URL allowlist enforcement pattern exists in publish.ts (executePublishPipeline), attest.ts, and pay.ts — all three apply the same allowlist check before SSRF validation. scan.ts, verify.ts, react.ts,
dimension_notes_summary.ai_generated_debt: Most comments explain WHY not WHAT — security rationale comments in connect.ts (container/WSL2 chmod behavior), SSRF comment in url-validator.ts, D402 flow comments in pay.ts are genuinely useful.
dimension_notes_summary.incomplete_migration: Deprecated guards in src/toolkit/guards/: checkWriteRateLimit(), recordWrite() in write-rate-limit.ts; checkDedup(), recordPublish() in dedup-guard.ts; checkTipSpendCap(), recordTip() in tip-spend-cap
dimension_notes_summary.package_organization: src/toolkit/ is well-organized: top-level (session, types, schemas, state-store, sdk-bridge, url-validator, index), tools/ (10 files), guards/ (6 files). Clear separation by role, well below the 10-fi
dimension_notes_summary.initialization_coupling: connect.ts uses lazy imports for SDK (`await import('@kynesyslabs/demosdk/websdk')`) and auth module — no module-level SDK side effects
dimension_notes_summary.design_coherence: pay() function is 217 lines with 9 numbered sequential steps — each step is distinct but the function body is the only natural home for D402 protocol orchestration; this is inherent protocol complexit
dimension_notes_summary.contract_coherence: All public tool functions (publish, reply, scan, tip, react, verify, attest, pay, discoverSources, connect) return typed ToolResult<T> or throw — return type annotations are accurate for all paths
dimension_notes_summary.logic_clarity: verify.ts: `throw new Error('unreachable')` after the for loop (line 62) — annotated with a comment explaining TypeScript requires it; the for loop always returns on its final iteration. This is inten
dimension_notes_summary.type_safety: schemas.ts: bidirectional compile-time type sync assertions (Forward: z.output<Schema> extends Interface, Reverse: Interface extends z.input<Schema>) for all 10 tool input types — strong positive patt

review_issue_summaries.summary: connect.ts dynamically imports src/lib/auth.js breaking toolkit's framework-agnostic boundary
Module-level mutable Map cache in discover-sources.ts persists across test runs and creates shared process state
feed-parser.ts is a shared parsing utility in tools/ rather than a proper utility/shared layer
discoverSources() docstring says session is not required but function signature requires DemosSession
pay-spend-cap.ts exports separate checkPaySpendCap/recordPayment functions alongside reservePaySpend, inconsistent with peers' checkAndRecord* pattern
pay.ts contains an inline dynamic import of safeParse inside safeReadBody, breaking the static-imports-at-top convention
connectSdk() wraps all internal exceptions as AUTH_FAILED regardless of actual failure cause
sdk-bridge.ts apiCall() converts network errors to plain string, losing error type information
connectSdk() understates its responsibility — it loads wallet credentials, connects SDK, and authenticates, not just connects
DemosSession exposes 12 public fields read directly by tools, making it a transparent property bag rather than a behavior-bearing session object
@anthropic-ai/sdk is a heavy production dep (~6MB) with zero direct imports in src/toolkit/ — burdens future toolkit packaging
checkBlockedIpv4 destructures [a, b, c, d] but c and d are never used in any blocking condition
verify.ts retry loop structure uses off-by-one index arithmetic and implicit fall-through that requires careful reading to verify correctness
reservePaySpend duplicates the amount validation from checkPaySpendCap with identical logic and error messages
scan() accepts a domain filter option but fetchFeed() silently ignores it in the API path
verify() silently returns unconfirmed for transactions outside the 50-post feed window — callers cannot distinguish 'not confirmed' from 'outside search window'
scan() uses a throw-then-catch seam in fetchFeed() where returning an error result directly would be cleaner
scan() silently drops the domain filter with no regression test — the broken behavior could be 'fixed' or changed without test failure
discover-sources.ts has a module-level Map cache (catalogCache) that persists across test runs and can cause cross-test pollution
connect() critical error paths (symlink rejection, mode-600 failure, container detection fallback) lack dedicated targeted tests
reply() validates input outside withToolWrapper — session.touch() not called and latencyMs is 0 on validation failure
scan/verify/react/tip make outbound API calls without SSRF validation applied to sibling tools (attest/pay/publish)
isDemosError inline check in tool-wrapper.ts catch block duplicates the exported isDemosErrorLike() in the same file
safeReadBody in pay.ts uses dynamic import for safeParse instead of static import
Two active write-rate-limit implementations coexist: legacy src/lib/ (sync/file) and toolkit src/toolkit/guards/ (async/StateStore) — CLI runners still use old system
6 deprecated guard functions remain in public index.ts export surface without removal timeline
src/lib/ has 42 mixed-concern files in a flat layout — exceeds cohesion threshold by 4x
src/adapter-specs.ts is at the src/ root despite src/adapters/ subdirectory existing
DEFAULT_STATE_DIR reads homedir() at module import time in state-store.ts
scan() accepts domain filter option but fetchFeed() ignores it — filtering is never applied
BUNDLED_CATALOG_PATH uses regex string replace on import.meta.url — fragile, assumes fixed directory depth
scan() accepts opts.domain in its contract but never applies the filter — silently no-ops
getAuthToken() and getSigningHandle() are getter-named but call checkExpired() which mutates _expired state
connectSdk() re-checks `Object.keys(connectOpts).length > 0` to decide which connectWallet overload to call, but connectOpts is only populated when `algorithm !== 'ed25519'`
safeReadBody() uses double-nested try/catch with async import inside the inner catch — success/failure paths are hard to follow
publishHivePost txHash extraction uses 6 chained `(result as any)?.` expressions — no type protection on on-chain confirmation path
backoff.ts uses non-null assertions (!) on lastResult and lastResult.error after loop — TypeScript cannot verify safety

dimension_notes_summary.cross_module_architecture: One import cycle exists: src/lib/extensions.ts -> src/lib/util/extensions.ts -> src/plugins/calibrate-plugin.ts. This is a 3-file cycle through a re-export shim.
dimension_notes_summary.high_level_elegance: Clear domain decomposition: toolkit/ (framework-agnostic tools), lib/ (business logic), adapters/ (framework bridges), plugins/ (session loop extensions), reactive/ (event system), actions/ (executors
dimension_notes_summary.convention_outlier: Naming is consistently camelCase throughout all directories — no snake_case drift detected.
dimension_notes_summary.error_consistency: Toolkit layer uses consistent Result pattern: ok/err with typed DemosError codes. All tools return ToolResult<T>.
dimension_notes_summary.naming_quality: Tool names are domain-specific and descriptive: publish, scan, verify, attest, discoverSources, pay.
dimension_notes_summary.abstraction_fitness: Wrapper rate is very low (0.014) — 3 pass-through wrappers out of 212 function signatures.
dimension_notes_summary.dependency_health: @anthropic-ai/sdk remains a production dependency despite only being used via dynamic import in src/lib/llm/llm-provider.ts. Could be devDependency since it's lazy-loaded.
dimension_notes_summary.low_level_elegance: checkAndAppend in state-helpers.ts is a clean generic with proper lock/prune/check/append flow.
dimension_notes_summary.mid_level_elegance: Toolkit tools compose cleanly: withToolWrapper -> validateInput -> domain logic -> ok/err return.
dimension_notes_summary.test_strategy: 1815 tests across 125 suites — strong overall volume.
dimension_notes_summary.api_surface_coherence: All toolkit tools follow consistent pattern: (session, opts?) -> Promise<ToolResult<T>>.
dimension_notes_summary.authorization_consistency: SSRF validation is consistently applied across attest, pay, publish, and connect (apiBaseUrl).
dimension_notes_summary.ai_generated_debt: isDemosErrorLike duplication has been resolved — single isDemosError in types.ts.
dimension_notes_summary.incomplete_migration: CLI runners (session-runner.ts, event-runner.ts) still import deprecated legacy write-rate-limit from src/lib/.
dimension_notes_summary.package_organization: src/lib/ has 41 files with mixed concerns — 4x the 10-file threshold. Partially restructured (auth/, llm/, attestation/, scoring/ subdirectories created) but 33+ flat files remain.
dimension_notes_summary.initialization_coupling: _isContainer module-level cache in connect.ts reads FS on first call — process-invariant caching is correct but fragile in test environments.
dimension_notes_summary.design_coherence: declarative-engine.ts at 1534 LOC with monster function flag — large but single-responsibility (YAML spec interpreter).
dimension_notes_summary.contract_coherence: getWriteRateRemaining now properly uses lock and filters by time window — previously flagged race condition appears resolved.
dimension_notes_summary.logic_clarity: verify() returning ok({confirmed:false}) on retry exhaustion is semantically ambiguous — still open issue.
dimension_notes_summary.type_safety: discover-sources.ts parses catalog entries with inline Record<string,unknown> casts — no typed interface for catalog JSON structure (uses CatalogEntrySchema for validation but falls back to as Catalog
